ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendments And Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Dan Rubel (Petitioner). David Baer for Donald Crabtree.

The Court took the Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Amendments, etc. off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2012 Vacated

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendments And Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Continued To Apr-24-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Baer for Donald Crabtree (Trustee). Brian Connors for Daniel Rubel.

Mr. Baer stated that the settlement amount would be taken off the top and that the Residual Beneficiaries shares would be reduced. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to why the Residual Beneficiaries need not receive notice of the settlement agreement and whether the Court has authority to require that notice be given. The Court stated that it was not prepared to approve dismissal of the action without notice to all of the Beneficiary to reduce their shares. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. was continued to April 24, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Court to do more research on whether it has jurisdiciton.

Judge Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Continued To Apr-09-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Petitioner D.R. Rubel'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation Between Parties And For Injuctive Relief; P & A In Support; Declaration Of B.F. Connors In Support.
Off Calendar Dec-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Daniel Rubel (moving party). David Baer for Donald Crabtree (Trustee).

Mr. Baer presented oral argument to support his position. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the Tentative Ruling as follow: Petitioner Daniel Rubel's Motion to Enforce Stipulation Between Parties and for Injunctive Relief is granted in part and denied in part. Respondent Donald Crabtree shall produce a copy of the escrow instructions, closing papers from the sale of 393 Marina Blvd., San Francisco, and verification of the deposit of the net proceeds of sale of the Marina Blvd. property to Daniel Rubel within five days. Petitioner's request that any net proceeds from the sale of the house which have already been spent be returned is denied. Donald Crabtree shall hold and maintain all remaining net proceeds until resolution of the contest of the Third and Fourth Amendments is decided. The request for an injunction is denied. The request for an evidentiary hearing is denied. There are no disputed issues of fact. The dispute is regarding the language of the stipulation. The interpretation of the stipulation is a question of law for the court. Counsel agreed to certain expenses being paid out of the trust if parties to the stipulation agree in writing. Mr. Connor is to prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: N/A

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-09-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Daniel Rubel. Paul Melbostad for Donald Crabtree.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The tentative stands as follows: Daniel Rubel's Motion for Summary Adjudication of the Fourth Cause of Action is denied. There are triable issues of fact as to the existence of a contract, performance under the contract, and damages. In addition, summary judgment is denied based upon CCP section 437c(e). The request for judicial notice of the Petition to Determine Validity of Trust, etc is granted. The objection to the Declaration of David W. Baer filed December 5, 2011 is sustained, except with regard to Exhibit 1 attached to the Declaration which was not produced by Daniel Rubel prior to December 1, 2011. However, Exhibit 1 is not determinative of the Court's ruling in this matter. The Court will prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Miramontes.

Respondent D. Crabtree'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgement On The Pleadings As To The Fourth And Fifth Causes Of Action Of The Petition.
Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Denied

Respondent D. Crabtree'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgement On The Pleadings As To The Third Cause Oif Action Of The Petition.
Off Calendar Dec-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Daniel Rubel. Paul Melbostad and David Baer for Donald Crabtree. Eugene Hom for San Francisco SPCA.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court granted the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to the 3rd Cause of Action with 30 days leave to amend (Mr. Bear is to prepare the order). The Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to the 4th Cause of Action and the 5th Action was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Joan Cesano, CSR#2590. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: C. Garcia.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Continuance Of Trial And All Discovery Cut Off Dates; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Text Ruling

Petitioner D. R. Rubel'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Attorney'S
Off Calendar Oct-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Petitioner. Paul Melbostad for Donald Crabtree (Respondent). David Baer (Co-Counsel) for Donald Crabtree (Respondent). Nancy Lofdahl (Beneficiary) also present. Alan Herzfeld (Paul Melbostad's Associate) also present.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: 1) Petitioner's motion to disqualify John A. Hartog, individually from representing Donald Crabtree, Respondent and Trustee, is granted.
2) The motion to disqualify attorneys other than John A. Hartog at Hartog & Associates, Inc., including David W. Baer is denied. 3) Petitioner's request to disqualify the firm of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran, LLP is also denied. Mr. Bear is to prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Mr. Connors stated that notice to Jeffrey Abuan has been done and that a Notice and Acknowlegment of Receipt was filed. Nancy Lofdahl stated her appearance for the record and informed the Court that Jeanie Rubel predeceased Norman in a car accident four of five years ago.
